nodes.context: Mexican drug trafficking organization led by Rafael Caro Quintero, operating mainly in Sonora and Chihuahua.
Defunct Mexican drug cartel founded by Rafael Caro Quintero and others.
Armed wing of the Sinaloa Cartel, involved in disputes with Caborca Cartel.
Major Mexican drug cartel, rival of Caborca Cartel, with factions like Los Salazar and Los Chapitos.
Mexican drug cartel allied with Caborca Cartel to dispute territory in Sonora and Chihuahua.
Criminal cell led by Rodrigo Páez Quintero, nephew of Rafael Caro Quintero, associated with Caborca Cartel.
Criminal cell related to the Sinaloa Cartel, involved in conflict with Los Páez.
Faction of the Sinaloa Cartel led by the sons of Joaquín 'El Chapo' Guzmán, disputed territory with Caborca Cartel.
Mexican drug cartel listed as an ally of Caborca Cartel in the infobox.

edges.context: Caborca Cartel was founded by Rafael Caro Quintero, founder of the defunct Guadalajara Cartel.
Caborca Cartel has a bloody dispute with Los Salazar, the armed wing of the Sinaloa Cartel.
Caborca Cartel disputes territory in Sonora with the Sinaloa Cartel and its factions.
Caborca Cartel allied with the Juárez Cartel to dispute areas of Sonora and Chihuahua.
Los Páez is a criminal cell led by Rafael Caro Quintero's nephew, associated with Caborca Cartel.
Conflict between Los Páez and Los Cazadores (related to Sinaloa Cartel) caused increased homicides in Sonora.
Los Páez and Los Salazar are in conflict as part of the larger Caborca-Sinaloa rivalry.
Los Cazadores is a cell related to the Sinaloa Cartel.
Los Salazar is identified as the armed wing of the Sinaloa Cartel.
Los Chapitos is a faction of the Sinaloa Cartel led by the sons of Joaquín Guzmán.
Caborca Cartel disputes territory in Sonora with Los Chapitos faction.
Listed as an ally in the infobox.
